requiredMachineState.description: Canonical persisted one-shot init result for zero-Pact Java/Spring provider repos. It must capture whether init completed, was not justified, was blocked by insufficient boundary evidence, or was redirected because existing Pact evidence already exists; identify the provider candidate and HTTP surface; record existing Pact evidence honestly; explain the narrow bootstrap plan and writes; and persist proof level, artifact-source status, verification grounding, what is still unproven, blockers, and the correct next step without implying runnable verification.

outcomeTaxonomy.description: pact-init must classify its one-shot bootstrap outcome using this narrow verdict set. `init-completed` means deterministic bootstrap was written, not that Pact verification is already grounded or runnable.

proofSemantics.description: Zero-Pact init completion must persist `proofLevel: bootstrap-only`, explicit unresolved artifact-source status when no broker or local pact evidence exists, and a next-step type that reflects the exact missing proof instead of greenwashing readiness.

bootstrapBaselines.description: Bootstrap scaffold shape must come from these pack-owned init baselines rather than ad hoc freeform generation. The provider harness must stay artifact-source neutral unless repo evidence already grounds broker or local pact inputs.

allowedRepoWrites: minimal Pact provider test dependency patching in pom.xml or build.gradle when clearly needed
one deterministic provider verification harness derived from the pack-owned baseline
an optional neutral provider-state support shell derived from the pack-owned baseline when justified
forbiddenRepoWrites: invented concrete @State(...) names without real pact grounding
claims that provider verification is already proven, runnable, or passing
writePolicy.description: pact-init may write only narrow provider-side bootstrap artifacts and must leave the repo in an honest bootstrap-only state unless existing repo evidence already grounds Pact verification.

stopConditions.description: Meaningful Pact provider evidence already exists, so init must redirect to the normal verification track instead of scaffolding a parallel setup.
The repo does not provide enough boundary evidence to choose a provider under contract honestly.
The repo exposes weak or internal-only HTTP seams, so Pact bootstrap is not justified right now.

summaryState.description: Human-readable Pact init summary only. Canonical machine state stays in init-state.json. The summary must make bootstrap-only outcomes, unproven areas, blockers, and next-step grounding needs obvious to an engineer.

allowedSharedData: Spring controller and request mapping evidence
